subject: Add chart for redis cluster
commit_message:
Add chart for redis cluster

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-417
Change-Id: I848f005a6e9daee09e50ddba9cd16527ec1e17d6
Signed-off-by: Lusheng Ji <lji@research.att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

(14 comments)

Overall the redis cluster looks good.
It is not exactly according to the charts structure we try to create, but it will be easy enough to adjust.
Please take a look at examples in so, appc, helm/starters/onap-app (this is a template) etc.
I believe the existing file "pod.yaml" is no longer relevant, instead maybe better put here the manifests from amsterdam release?

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Thank for the review.  Before I start to make patch, I am a little confused by the global.* values.  Are they defined in dcaegen2's own chart, a global node in dcagen2's values.yaml, or there is a place defining these values for the whole ONAP?  It seems projects like aai define these globals in their own values.yaml file.

Patch Set 1:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Thank for the review.  Before I start to make patch, I am a little
 > confused by the global.* values.  Are they defined in dcaegen2's
 > own chart, a global node in dcagen2's values.yaml, or there is a
 > place defining these values for the whole ONAP?  It seems projects
 > like aai define these globals in their own values.yaml file.

The global values provide another level of control. Let's say you want to change some value everywhere - like location of your persistence directory. you can use global value. If global value is not defined, you can use local value. Example:
  hostPath:
    path: {{ .Values.global.persistence.mountPath | default .Values.persistence.mountPath }}/{{ .Release.Namespace }}/{{ .Values.persistence.mountSubPath }}

global values can be defined in chart yaml, in parent yaml, in onap yaml and by providing parameter to helm install command (using --set).
I sent you the processed chart in email...
